title: Cracca discolor
intro:
I couldn't find information on the plant Cracca discolor. However, based on the name 'Cracca', it seems to be related to the plant "Cracca" or "Cracca montana" which is also known as Wild licorice or Black licorice or "Glycyrrhiza yunnanensis" (Cracca yunnanensis), or "Glycyrrhiza lepidota". 

Cracca discolor, also known as Wild licorice or Black licorice, is a perennial plant that belongs to the legume family. The roots of this plant have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, offering various health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, as well as soothing digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Cracca discolor include glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Cracca discolor include teas, tinctures, and extracts, which are used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions.
